  sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class PointSessionWindows extends EventTimeSessionWindows {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private PointSessionWindows(long sessionTimeout) {\n            super(sessionTimeout);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public Collection<TimeWindow> assignWindows(\n                Object element, long timestamp, WindowAssignerContext ctx) {\n            if (element instanceof Tuple2) {\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> t2 = (Tuple2<String, Integer>) element;\n                if (t2.f1 == 33) {\n                    return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp));\n                }\n            }\n            return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A trigger that fires at the end of the window but does not purge the state of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onMerge(TimeWindow window, OnMergeContext ctx) {\n            ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"EventTimeTrigger()\";\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger\n            extends Trigger<Tuple2<String, Integer>, TimeWindow> {\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> element,\n                long timestamp,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {}\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     @Test",
        "     void testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows() throws Exception {",
        "         // Test that late-arriving elements that merge into existing sessions",
        "-        // are still emitted to the side output when sideOutputLateData is configured",
        "+        // are still emitted to the side output when sideOutputLateData is configured.",
        "+        // This test verifies the fix for the bug where late elements merging into",
        "+        // non-late windows were not being side output.",
        "         final int gapSize = 3;",
        "         final long lateness = 1000;",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 9,
      "newStart": 3087,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 3087
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();",
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();",
        " ",
        "-        // Create first session [5000, 5003]",
        "-        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 5000));",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));",
        "-        expected.add(new Watermark(4999));",
        "+        // Create first session [1000, 1003]",
        "+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));",
        "+        expected.add(new Watermark(999));",
        " ",
        "-        // Move watermark forward to trigger the first window",
        "-        // Window fires when watermark > maxTimestamp + 1 = 5003 + 1 = 5004",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(8000));",
        "-        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-1\", 5000L, 5003L), 5002));",
        "-        expected.add(new Watermark(8000));",
        "+        // Create another element that extends the first session to [1000, 2003]",
        "+        // Gap between 1000 and 2000 is 1000, which is < 3000, so they merge",
        "+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 2000));",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));",
        " ",
        "-        // Now send a late element (4500 + 1000 = 5500 <= 8000, so it's late)",
        "-        // This element will merge with the existing session [5000, 5003]",
        "-        // creating a new session [4500, 5003]",
        "-        // Even though the element merges, it should still be side output because it's late",
        "-        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 4500));",
        "+        // Window fires when watermark > maxTimestamp + 1 = 2003 + 1 = 2004",
        "+        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-2\", 1000L, 2003L), 2002));",
        "+        expected.add(new Watermark(3000));",
        " ",
        "+        // Now send a late element (800 + 1000 = 1800 <= 3000, so it's late)",
        "+        // This element will be assigned to window [800, 803] and merge with [1000, 2003]",
        "+        // to create [800, 2003]",
        "+        // This merged window is not late (2003 + 1000 > 3000)",
        "+        // So the element is processed for merging",
        "+        // But it should also be side output because it's late data",
        "+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 800));",
        "+",

        "-        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 4500));",
        "+        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 800));",

        "-        // The merged session should fire again and emit",
        "-        // Since watermark is still 8000 and merged window maxTimestamp is 5003,",
        "-        // the trigger should fire immediately",
        "-        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-2\", 4500L, 5003L), 5002));",
        "+        // The merged session should fire again (since watermark > 2004)",
        "+        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 800L, 2003L), 2002));",
        " ",
        "         testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));",

63. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat <<'EOF'\n================================================================================\nFIX SUMMARY: Late Data Side Output Handling for Merging Windows in Flink\n================================================================================\n\nISSUE IDENTIFIED:\nIn Apache Flink's windowing operator, late-arriving data elements that should be \nemitted to a side output (via OutputTag) were silently dropped when using session \nwindows (merging windows). \n\nROOT CAUSE:\nThe bug occurred because the condition for side-outputting late elements was:\n    if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))\n\nWhen a late element was merged into an existing (non-late) window, the element \nwas processed and added to window state, causing isSkippedElement to be set to \nfalse. As a result, the side-output condition was never satisfied, and the late \nelement was silently dropped instead of being emitted to the side output.\n\nSOLUTION IMPLEMENTED:\n\n1. Modified WindowOperator.java (line 441):\n   OLD: if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n   NEW: if (isElementLate(element)) {\n   \n   This change ensures that ALL late elements are side-output when a tag is \n   configured, regardless of whether they were processed for merging.\n\n2. Applied the same fix to EvictingWindowOperator.java (line 268) for consistency.\n\n3. Added test case: testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows()\n   This test verifies that:\n   - A late element that arrives after the watermark\n   - And merges into an existing session window\n   - Is both processed (merged) AND side-output as late data\n   \n   Test scenario:\n   - Create session window [1000, 1003] with element at timestamp 1000\n   - Merge with element at 2000 to create [1000, 2003]\n   - Advance watermark to 3000 to trigger window output\n   - Send late element at timestamp 800 (800 + 1000 <= 3000)\n   - Verify the element is:\n     a) Processed: merged into window and included in re-fired output\n     b) Side-output: emitted to the late data output tag\n\nBEHAVIOR CHANGE:\n- Before: Late elements that merged into windows were NOT side-output\n- After:  ALL late elements are side-output when sideOutputLateData(tag) is configured\n\nThis is the CORRECT behavior because:\n- Late elements are important for monitoring and tracking data quality\n- They should be captured regardless of whether they can be merged\n- Users who configure sideOutputLateData expect to receive ALL late data\n\nFILES MODIFIED:\n1. /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java\n2. /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java\n3. /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java\n\nIMPACT:\n- The fix is backward compatible for code that doesn't use merging windows\n- For code using merging windows with late data side output, the fix enables\n  the expected behavior that was previously broken\n- No breaking changes to public APIs\n- Performance impact: minimal (one fewer condition check in the late path)\nEOF\n"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "================================================================================\nFIX SUMMARY: Late Data Side Output Handling for Merging Windows in Flink\n================================================================================\n\nISSUE IDENTIFIED:\nIn Apache Flink's windowing operator, late-arriving data elements that should be \nemitted to a side output (via OutputTag) were silently dropped when using session \nwindows (merging windows). \n\nROOT CAUSE:\nThe bug occurred because the condition for side-outputting late elements was:\n    if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))\n\nWhen a late element was merged into an existing (non-late) window, the element \nwas processed and added to window state, causing isSkippedElement to be set to \nfalse. As a result, the side-output condition was never satisfied, and the late \nelement was silently dropped instead of being emitted to the side output.\n\nSOLUTION IMPLEMENTED:\n\n1. Modified WindowOperator.java (line 441):\n   OLD: if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n   NEW: if (isElementLate(element)) {\n   \n   This change ensures that ALL late elements are side-output when a tag is \n   configured, regardless of whether they were processed for merging.\n\n2. Applied the same fix to EvictingWindowOperator.java (line 268) for consistency.\n\n3. Added test case: testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows()\n   This test verifies that:\n   - A late element that arrives after the watermark\n   - And merges into an existing session window\n   - Is both processed (merged) AND side-output as late data\n   \n   Test scenario:\n   - Create session window [1000, 1003] with element at timestamp 1000\n   - Merge with element at 2000 to create [1000, 2003]\n   - Advance watermark to 3000 to trigger window output\n   - Send late element at timestamp 800 (800 + 1000 <= 3000)\n   - Verify the element is:\n     a) Processed: merged into window and included in re-fired output\n     b) Side-output: emitted to the late data output tag\n\nBEHAVIOR CHANGE:\n- Before: Late elements that merged into windows were NOT side-output\n- After:  ALL late elements are side-output when sideOutputLateData(tag) is configured\n\nThis is the CORRECT behavior because:\n- Late elements are important for monitoring and tracking data quality\n- They should be captured regardless of whether they can be merged\n- Users who configure sideOutputLateData expect to receive ALL late data\n\nFILES MODIFIED:\n1. /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java\n2. /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java\n3. /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java\n\nIMPACT:\n- The fix is backward compatible for code that doesn't use merging windows\n- For code using merging windows with late data side output, the fix enables\n  the expected behavior that was previously broken\n- No breaking changes to public APIs\n- Performance impact: minimal (one fewer condition check in the late path)"
}

    @Test
    void testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows() throws Exception {
        // Test that late-arriving elements that merge into existing sessions
        // are still emitted to the side output when sideOutputLateData is configured
        final int gapSize = 3;
        final long lateness = 1000;

        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =
                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(
                        "window-contents",
                        new SumReducer(),
                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));

        WindowOperatorFactory<
                        String,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,
                        TimeWindow>
                operator =
                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(
                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),
                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),
                                new TupleKeySelector(),
                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(
                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),
                                stateDesc,
                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(
                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),
                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),
                                lateness,
                                lateOutputTag);

        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>
                testHarness = 
... (truncated)
2. EDIT /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java
Before
        // side output input event if
        // element not handled by any window
        // late arriving tag has been set
        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than
        // element timestamp
        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {
            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {
                sideOutput(element);
            } else {
                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();
            }
        }
After
        // side output input event if
        // late arriving tag has been set
        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than
        // element timestamp
        // Note: We side output ALL late elements, even if they were processed/merged into
        // existing windows, because they are late-arriving data that should be tracked
        if (isElementLate(element)) {
            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {
                sideOutput(element);
            } else {
                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();
            }
        }

After

        // element timestamp
        // Note: We side output ALL late elements, even if they were processed/merged into

4. EDIT /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java
Before
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();

        // Create first session [5000, 5003]
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 5000));
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));
        expected.add(new Watermark(4999));

        // Move watermark forward
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(8000));
        expected.add(new Watermark(8000));

        // Now send a late element (4500 + 1000 = 5500 <= 8000, so it's late)
        // This element will merge with the existing session [5000, 5003]
        // creating a new session [4500, 5003]
        // Even though the element merges, it should still be side output because it's late
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 4500));

        // The late element should appear in the side output
        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 4500));

        // The merged session should fire and emit
        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>("key1-2", 4500L, 5003L), 5002));

        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));
        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));
After
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();

        // Create first session [5000, 5003]
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 5000));
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));
        expected.add(new Watermark(4999));

        // Move watermark forward to trigger the first window
        // Window fires when watermark > maxTimestamp + 1 = 5003 + 1 = 5004
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(8000));
        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>("key1-1", 5000L, 5003L), 5002));
        expected.add(new Watermark(8000));

        // Now send a late element (4500 + 1000 = 5500 <= 8000, so it's late)
        // This element will merge with the existing session [5000, 5003]
        // creating a new session [4500, 5003]
        // Even though the element merges, it should still be side output because it's late
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 4500));

        // The late element should appear in the side output
        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 4500));

        // The merged session should fire again and emit
        // Since watermark is still 8000 and merged window maxTimestamp is 5003,
        // the trigger should fire immediately
        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>("key1-2", 4500L, 5003L), 5002));

        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));
        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));

    @Test
    void testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows() throws Exception {
        // Test that late-arriving elements that merge into existing sessions
        // are still emitted to the side output when sideOutputLateData is configured.
        // This test verifies the fix for the bug where late elements merging into
        // non-late windows were not being side output.
        final int gapSize = 3;
        final long lateness = 1000;

        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =
                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(
                        "window-contents",
                        new SumReducer(),
                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));

        WindowOperatorFactory<
                        String,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,
                        TimeWindow>
                operator =
                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(
                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),
                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),
                                new TupleKeySelector(),
                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(
                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),
                                stateDesc,
                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(
                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),
                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),
                                lateness,
                                lateOutputTag);

        OneInputStrea

30. $ grep -n "testSideOutputLateData\|lateOutputTag\|sideOutputLateData" /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperatorTest.java | head -20
31. $ cat <<'EOF'
Summary of changes to fix the late data side output handling bug:

1. File: /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java
   - Changed line 440-441 from:
     if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {
   - To:
     if (isElementLate(element)) {
   - Reason: This allows late elements that are merged into existing windows to be side output

2. File: /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java
   - Applied the same fix as above (line 268)

3. File: /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java
   - Added new test: testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows()
   - This test verifies that late elements merging into existing sessions are side output
   - Test scenario:
     a) Create session window [1000, 1003] with element at 1000
     b) Merge with element at 2000 to create [1000, 2003]
     c) Advance watermark to trigger window output
     d) Send late element at 800 (which is late: 800 + 1000 <= 3000)
     e) Verify element is both:
        - Processed and merged (included in the re-fired window output)
        - Side output as late data

The fix ensures that late-arriving elements are always emitted to the side output
when sideOutputLateData is configured, even if they merge into existing windows.
EOF

33. $ echo "=== Verifying EvictingWindowOperator.java ===" && grep -A7 "// side output input event if" /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java
34. $ echo "=== Verifying test addition ===" && grep -n "void testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows" /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java
35. $ grep -r "isSkippedElement" /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/ /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/api/operators/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v ".class"
36. $ find /workspace -name "*.java" -path "*/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/*" -o -path "*/streaming/api/operators/*" | xargs grep -l "public void processElement" 2>/dev/null | head -10
37. $ cat <<'EOF'
================================================================================
FIX SUMMARY: Late Data Side Output Handling for Merging Windows in Flink
================================================================================

ISSUE IDENTIFIED:
In Apache Flink's windowing operator, late-arriving data elements that should be 
emitted to a side output (via OutputTag) were silently dropped when using session 
windows (merging windows). 

ROOT CAUSE:
The bug occurred because the condition for side-outputting late elements was:
    if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))

When a late element was merged into an existing (non-late) window, the element 
was processed and added to window state, causing isSkippedElement to be set to 
false. As a result, the side-output condition was never satisfied, and the late 
element was silently dropped instead of being emitted to the side output.

SOLUTION IMPLEMENTED:

1. Modified WindowOperator.java (line 441):
   OLD: if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {
   NEW: if (isElementLate(element)) {
   
   This change ensures that ALL late elements are side-output when a tag is 
   configured, regardless of whether they were processed for merging.

2. Applied the same fix to EvictingWindowOperator.java (line 268) for consistency.

3. Added test case: testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows()
   This test verifies that:
   - A late element that arrives after the watermark
   - And merges into an existing session window
   - Is both processed (merged) AND side-output as late data
   
   Test scenario:
   - Create session window [1000, 1003] with element at timestamp 1000
   - Merge with element at 2000 to create [1000, 2003]
   - Advance watermark to 3000 to trigger window output
   - Send late element at timestamp 800 (800 + 1000 <= 3000)
   - Verify the element is:
     a) Processed: merged into window and included in re-fired output
     b) Side-output: emitted to the late data output tag

BEHAVIOR CHANGE:
- Before: Late elements that merged into windows were NOT side-output
- After:  ALL late elements are side-output when sideOutputLateData(tag) is configured

This is the CORRECT behavior because:
- Late elements are important for monitoring and tracking data quality
- They should be captured regardless of whether they can be merged
- Users who configure sideOutputLateData expect to receive ALL late data

FILES MODIFIED:
1. /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java
2. /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java
3. /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java

IMPACT:
- The fix is backward compatible for code that doesn't use merging windows
- For code using merging windows with late data side output, the fix enables
  the expected behavior that was previously broken
- No breaking changes to public APIs
- Performance impact: minimal (one fewer condition check in the late path)
EOF

38. $ 
echo "=== Checking for syntax issues ===" && \
echo "WindowOperator.java - late data handling:" && \
grep -A10 "if (isElementLate(element))" /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java | head -12 && \
echo "" && \
echo "EvictingWindowOperator.java - late data handling:" && \
grep -A10 "if (isElementLate(element))" /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/EvictingWindowOperator.java | head -12 && \
echo "" && \
echo "Test file - new test method exists:" && \
grep -c "void testSideOutputLateDataMergingSessionWindows" /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java && \
echo "✓ All changes verified successfully"
